_provenance: v4 motion-packet rewrite 2026-05-16 per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Whimsy subsection) + §41 SPRINT_LOG checklist item 8. Whimsy surfaces the rights memo (marginalia anchor) and the 2018 property-transfer fact (civic-records hobby) without declaring the fair-hearing doctrine as the winning argument. The 'fair-hearing argument can sing' calibration anchor is moved out of the recruitment state and kept as a post-recruitment posture line; recruitment itself becomes a literal hand-off of two pieces of paper (the memo and the records folder). Two new decoy-decision states land D3 (standing/wrong-party) and D4 (overbroad remedy) as named auxiliary offers; both write the corresponding decoy_* bool per PROPOSAL §3.2. Address forms unchanged: 'Dr. A. Cula' pre-recruit, bare 'Cula' post-recruit.
_anti_ai_voice_pass: No contrastive antithesis. Em-dashes capped per Whimsy's declamatory register (single sanctioned per line). No scrub-list vocabulary. The 'folder of who-owns-what' civic-records line is the v4 in-fiction justification for D3 surfacing here.

states._comment: v4 motion-packet rewrite. 2026-05-19 critique F8: D3 (standing/wrong-party) surfacing moved from Whimsy's voice to Crab's lane (see crab.json::crab_property_transfer_offer). Whimsy now points AT Crab for the ownership question and stays in his rhetorical lane. on_dismiss still sets surfaced_property_transfer (the mechanical flag — preserves the v4 surface-on-Whimsy-recruit gameplay path; the existing evidence card in evidence_ch1.json is the alternate setter). Crab's new state owns the actual fact-disclosure and the D3 decoy options. Address form 'Dr. A. Cula' pre-recruit.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Whimsy. Cula, Pig & Swine. We need a second voice in court tomorrow.

lines: Dr. A. Cula. Sit. Have you anything beyond procedural vibes, or am I to be recruited on atmosphere?

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Notice went to an address the client left two years ago. The objection is in the papers; the record does not show it. Hearing at fourteen hundred.

lines: Ah. A wrong-door file, with the right door also in the file. I have heard worse music.
Marginalia in espresso. Half the argument is already mine — whichever half you decide it is.
Before the music, a thread. Crab will want to ask who owns the building today. Owners change. Notices, alas, do not.
I will lend my voice tomorrow at fourteen hundred. The shape you bring, I will sing.

states._comment: 2026-05-19 critique F8: D3 (standing/wrong-party) ownership moved from Whimsy to Crab (see crab.json::crab_property_transfer_offer). State retained as a tombstone with trigger:'false' so the runtime never fires it; a future cleanup pass can remove it once the migration test fixtures are regenerated. Whimsy's rhetorical-anchor lane is preserved; Crab's notice-the-inconsistency lane now carries the standing question.

v4 NEW state — D4 (overbroad remedy) decision moment per PROPOSAL §1.2. Whimsy's rhetorical-flourish offer ('for theatre we could ask for the world'). The choice writes chapter1.decoy_overbroad_remedy. The 'remedy discipline (load-bearing)' rule from story.txt Beat 12 makes the procedural-reset-only ask the canonical remedy; the offer is the temptation, the discipline is the test. once:true. The trigger is intentionally permissive — Whimsy will offer the flourish whether or not the standing question has been resolved.
lines: While the file is open, Cula. The remedy ask.

For theatre we could ask for the world — permanent injunction, dismissal with prejudice, costs on the indemnity scale. Or we could ask only for what we need: the procedural reset, hearing rescheduled, merits preserved.
Both are sayable. One is winnable. Pick.

choices.text: Only what we need. Reset the hearing. The bench will not thank us for asking widely.
Ask widely. We can always retreat to the narrow remedy at oral argument.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F4:** Whimsy's Round 2 carrier — sympathetic stance. judge_b12_react_r2 has been reacting to the canonical soup-and-fork-of-bees metaphor since the v3 sweep, but the metaphor was never authored in Whimsy's voice. This state lands it. Style canon §1 supplies the soup metaphor verbatim; style canon §2 mandates one Wordsworth or Shakespeare cite per Phase 2 closing where Whimsy is on stage — Hamlet's 'the law's delay' fits the sympathetic register because it names the human cost of procedural injustice directly. Address form: 'Your Honor' (court-formal). once:true. **NOTE — dispatch:** the Round 2 court flow currently relies on the casebook system advancing casebook_judge_state; verify Whimsy's state fires after the player's Casebook move and before judge_b12_react_r2 when the court-round dispatch sprint lands.
lines: Your Honor. A fair hearing is like soup. If one side is given a spoon and the other a fork made of bees, the court must intervene.

Mrs. Sikorska sat at a kitchen table for three weeks with a docket that had already moved past her. Hamlet named the harm we are asking the court to undo — 'the law's delay' — and he meant precisely this: a notice that arrives at an envelope but not a person.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F4:** Whimsy's Round 2 carrier — blunt-procedural stance. Soup metaphor verbatim per style canon §1. Shakespeare cite: Hamlet's 'the insolence of office' — same speech as the sympathetic variant's 'law's delay', different complaint, suits the procedural-sequence framing. The cite names what produces this paper. Address form: 'Your Honor'. once:true.
lines: Your Honor. A fair hearing is like soup. If one side is given a spoon and the other a fork made of bees, the court must intervene.

The sequence here is plain. The address was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen with the landlord's countersignature; notice was served eleven years later to the address that had ceased to exist. Hamlet called this 'the insolence of office.' A fair hearing requires the office in question to consult its own files before serving them.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 critique F4:** Whimsy's Round 2 carrier — technical stance. Soup metaphor verbatim per style canon §1. Wordsworth cite from 'Lines Composed a Few Miles Above Tintern Abbey': 'we see into the life of things' — suits the documentary completeness the technical stance has placed before the bench (1962 lease, 1987 succession, 2019 renewal). The cite frames the landlord's failure as the absence of that patient attention. Address form: 'Your Honor'. once:true.
lines: Your Honor. A fair hearing is like soup. If one side is given a spoon and the other a fork made of bees, the court must intervene.

The record before this court spans sixty-four years — original lease, succession, renewal, all in the file. Wordsworth wrote that with patient attention, one sees 'into the life of things.' The landlord did not pay that attention. He served notice on a building the documents show stopped being his address eleven years ago. A fair hearing requires the office to look at its own ledger before it asks the court to enforce silence.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** Beat 7 dwell-prompt picker for Whimsy. Scaffolded from cula.json::cula_b7_whimsy_dwell_options. **Trigger change vs cula.json:** the original cula.json gate was `met_whimsy && !recruited_whimsy`, but whimsy.json::before_meeting sets BOTH flags on dismiss, leaving no met-but-not-recruited window — the dwell would never fire as gated. Re-gated post-recruit (bare 'Cula' address form per AGENTS.md) and bounded by !halina_arrived && !entered_court so dwells stay in the Café-Paragraf beat. State-level speaker:'cula'; options chain to reply states. **Soft issue:** picker may re-fire after all three replies exhaust because there is no dwell-completed flag — match the pattern used elsewhere in this file; if runner behaviour requires a hard guard, add chapter1.whimsy_b7_dwells_done on the third reply's dismiss.
states.speaker: cula
lines: [Whimsy has agreed. The espresso cup is between you. One more thing before going back to the office.]

choices.text: Whimsy. Why the café, and not the office?
Setting the law aside for a second: what is your read on this one?
Honest question: do you ever turn up at the office, or is this the rest of the lease?

states._comment: V1.5 post-recruitment progression. Gates on both recruited flags; fires while the client meeting is still pending. Whimsy registers the missing ingredient. Bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md §Address forms (post-recruit).
lines: Cula. We have procedure and rhetoric. What we do not yet have is a client in the room. That tends to be useful.

states._comment: V1.5 post-recruitment progression. Fires once archive research is complete and court_ready is still pending. Whimsy supplies the court-door framing from whimsy_ch01_019. Bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md §Address forms.
lines: Cula. We are not asking the court for miracles. Merely a doorway through which the client may be heard.

lines: Cula. Somewhere nearby, a right is being reduced to administration. Let us be irritating about it.

states._comment: Beat 13 post-victory exchange with Whimsy, paired with cula.json::cula_b13_whimsy_response. Theatrical-aesthete register; the judgment-as-artistic-specimen framing per story.txt Beat 13 ('proposes turning the judgment into a musical') — Cynic register, theatrical distancing dressed as flourish. 'A throughline, a reversal, and a satisfying last line' is correct literary analysis applied to a procedural court order; 'dramatic rights' is the laugh. **2026-05-24 promotion** of data/_drafts/nightly_design_pig_2026-05-14.json::whimsy_beat13.proposed_state line. Address form bare 'Cula' from Whimsy (post-recruit per AGENTS.md).
lines: Cula. The judgment has a throughline, a reversal, and a satisfying last line. I want the dramatic rights.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I will look forward to the score, Whimsy. The written closing first, if you can.

idle_flavor: A procedural argument without a metaphor is like coffee without water. Dry and slightly offensive.
